D9 BiologyL3 ApplyReference

Dog Dichromacy — Yellow-Blue World

dogs have only two cone classes (~430 + 555 nm) — equivalent to red-green colour-blind humans (deuteranopes); they see yellows and blues clearly, red and green merge

Dog cones peak around 430 nm (blue) and 555 nm (yellow-green) — no red receptor. A red ball on green grass is barely visible (both signal mid-cones similarly). Blue and yellow toys stand out sharply. Trainers exploit this: high-vis dog gear is blue/yellow, not red.

Toggle human vs dog view of the same scene (red ball on grass).
